subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Tokens
- **40 total tokens** — This includes all gem tokens and gold jokers.
- **7 emerald** — Green gem tokens.
- **7 diamond** — White gem tokens.
- **7 sapphire** — Blue gem tokens.
- **7 onyx** — Black gem tokens.
- **7 ruby** — Red gem tokens.
- **5 gold jokers** — Wild payment tokens.

#### Development Cards
- **90 total development cards** — Split into 3 levels.
- **40 Level 1 cards** — Lowest tier deck.
- **30 Level 2 cards** — Mid tier deck.
- **20 Level 3 cards** — Highest tier deck.

#### Noble Tiles
- **10 noble tiles** — Only players+1 are used each game.

#### Visibility Rules
- **Tableau visibility** — Purchased cards, bonuses, and prestige must remain visible to all players.
- **Token visibility** — Player token holdings are always open information.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Central Layout Steps
1. **Shuffle by level** — Shuffle Level 1, Level 2, and Level 3 decks separately.
2. **Stack decks vertically** — Place Level 1 bottom, Level 2 middle, Level 3 top.
3. **Reveal market** — Reveal 4 face-up cards from each level (12 total).
4. **Prepare nobles** — Shuffle noble tiles and reveal players+1 nobles.
5. **Sort token piles** — Make 6 visible piles by color (5 gem colors + gold).

#### Player-Count Setup Matrix
--- 2 Players ---
- **Nobles revealed** — 3 nobles (players+1).
- **Gem tokens removed** — Remove 3 per gem color.
- **Gem tokens remaining** — Leave 4 each of green/white/blue/black/red.
- **Gold tokens** — Keep all gold jokers.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Nobles revealed** — 4 nobles (players+1).
- **Gem tokens removed** — Remove 2 per gem color.
- **Gem tokens remaining** — Leave 5 each of green/white/blue/black/red.
- **Gold tokens** — Keep all gold jokers.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Nobles revealed** — 5 nobles (players+1).
- **Gem tokens removed** — Remove 0; use full gem supply.
- **Gem tokens remaining** — Keep all 7 of each gem color.
- **Gold tokens** — Keep all gold jokers.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting State
- **No starting assets** — Players begin with no tokens, no cards, and no nobles.
- **Clear tableau area** — Leave space to build purchased-card rows by color.

#### Tableau Organization
- **Sort by bonus color** — Group purchased cards so permanent discounts are easy to read.
- **Keep prestige visible** — Card point values must stay visible at all times.

#### Reserved Cards
- **Reserve limit** — You may hold up to 3 reserved cards.
- **Face-up reserve info** — If reserved from the market, identity was public when taken.
- **Blind reserve info** — If reserved from top of a deck, identity remains private until bought.

#### Open Information
- **Token counts are public** — Do not hide token colors or totals.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **First player** — The youngest player starts.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.
- **No pregame drafting** — There is no opening draft or opening purchase.
- **First action** — The starting player immediately takes one legal turn action.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Choose 1 action** — Take exactly one of the four legal actions.
2. **Refill market if needed** — If you took/reserved a face-up card, refill from the same level deck if possible.
3. **Check token limit** — If above 10 total tokens (including gold), return tokens down to 10.
4. **Check noble visit** — If your bonuses qualify for one or more nobles, take exactly one.
5. **Pass turn** — Continue clockwise.

#### Action Menu Reference
- **Take 3 different gems** — Three different non-gold colors.
- **Take 2 same-color gems** — Only if that color had at least 4 in supply before taking.
- **Reserve 1 card** — Reserve from market or blind from top of a level deck; take 1 gold if available.
- **Buy 1 card** — Buy from market or your reserve using tokens + bonuses + optional gold.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Take Tokens
- **Three-color action** — Take exactly 3 gem tokens of 3 different colors.
- **No duplicates** — You cannot take 2 of one color in the three-color action.
- **Double-take action** — Take exactly 2 tokens of one gem color.
- **Double-take condition** — The chosen color must have at least 4 tokens in supply before you take.

#### Reserve a Development Card
- **Reserve source** — Choose 1 face-up card OR draw top card of one level deck blindly.
- **Gold gain** — Take 1 gold joker if any remain.
- **No-gold reserve** — If gold is empty, you may still reserve the card.
- **Reserve cap** — You cannot hold more than 3 reserved cards.
- **No discard option** — Reserved cards stay in hand until purchased.

#### Purchase a Development Card
- **Buy source** — Buy 1 face-up card or 1 of your reserved cards.
- **Pay cost** — Spend required gem tokens after applying permanent bonus discounts.
- **Gold substitution** — Gold tokens may replace missing colors.
- **Token return** — Return all spent tokens (including gold) to supply.
- **Free buy possibility** — If bonuses cover full cost, pay 0 tokens.
- **Tableau gain** — Purchased card adds permanent bonus color and possibly prestige points.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Permanent Bonus Engine
- **Discount rule** — Each purchased card gives a permanent 1-color discount for future buys.
- **Stacking discounts** — Multiple bonuses of the same color stack.
- **Never spent** — Bonuses are permanent and not consumed when used.

#### Gold Jokers
- **Wild payment** — Gold substitutes for any gem color during purchase.
- **Not a bonus** — Gold does not count as permanent bonus.
- **No noble help** — Gold never helps meet noble requirements.

#### Noble Visits
- **Automatic check** — Noble eligibility is checked at end of your turn.
- **Mandatory claim** — If eligible, you must take a noble.
- **One noble max** — If eligible for several, choose exactly one.
- **Bonus-only condition** — Noble requirements use permanent bonuses only, never tokens.

#### Market Refill
- **Immediate refill** — Refill face-up slot immediately after a market card is taken/reserved.
- **Empty-deck exception** — If that level deck is empty, leave the slot empty.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **15+ prestige reached** — Trigger occurs as soon as any player reaches at least 15 prestige.
- **Finish the round** — Continue until all players have taken equal turns.

#### Final Scoring
- **Development points** — Count all prestige on purchased development cards.
- **Noble points** — Add 3 prestige for each noble tile you claimed.
- **No other VP sources** — Only development prestige + nobles matter.

#### Winner & Tiebreak
- **Winner** — Highest total prestige wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — If tied on prestige, tied player with fewer purchased development cards wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First Turns
- **Build discounts first** — Prioritize efficient Level 1 cards that widen your color engine.
- **Plan 2-turn lines** — Take tokens only when they convert into a likely purchase soon.
- **Track noble overlap** — Target bonus colors that match multiple visible nobles.

#### Reserve with Purpose
- **Deny key cards** — Reserve a card when it is perfect for an opponent’s engine.
- **Bridge exact costs** — Reserve when the gold joker creates a near-term purchase.
- **Protect your combo** — Reserve critical cards you cannot risk losing from market churn.

#### Token Discipline
- **Stay under 10 efficiently** — Avoid overfilling tokens if it forces bad returns.
- **Keep flexible colors** — Prefer token sets that preserve multiple buying options next turn.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Over-hoarding tokens** — Hitting 10 often forces wasteful returns and kills tempo.
- **Rushing Level 3 cards** — Expensive point cards too early usually stalls your engine.
- **Ignoring nobles** — Free 3-point swings go to opponents if you never align your colors.
- **Over-reserving** — Filling all 3 reserve slots with expensive cards reduces flexibility.
- **Confusing tokens vs bonuses** — Nobles require permanent bonuses, not loose tokens.
- **Illegal double-take** — Taking 2 same-color tokens when fewer than 4 existed before taking is not allowed.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Engine vs Points
- **Engine turn** — Buy low-point cards when they accelerate multiple future purchases.
- **Point turn** — Pivot to prestige when your discounts can buy scoring cards quickly.

#### Decision 2: Reserve for Denial vs Reserve for Speed
- **Denial reserve** — Take a card mainly to block an opponent’s immediate plan.
- **Speed reserve** — Take a card mainly to gain gold and complete your own buy line.

#### Decision 3: Narrow Noble Plan vs Flexible Noble Plan
- **Narrow path** — Commit to one noble for speed if market supports it.
- **Flexible path** — Build overlapping colors so two nobles remain realistic if market shifts.

metadata.notes: Schema v2.0 guide rewritten for skimmable scanning format from provided Splendor rulebook text.
